comparable_series: Elmore Leonard (voice register for the new run)

voice_register.base_voice: Elmore Leonard-style: lean, dialogue-forward prose. Scenes start late and end early. Spare description carrying only what the scene needs. Strong individuated character voice. No throat-clearing, no purple description, no authorial editorializing. Short to medium sentences, present-forward action. Leave out the parts readers skip. Period-accurate detail delivered through action and dialogue rather than exposition blocks.
voice_register.intrusion_voice: Reserved and rare. At moments of genuine weight — a man dying, a friend lost, the cost of the work — the prose may extend slightly, but never into lyricism. Restraint is the rule even at the lowest point.
voice_register.intrusion_allocation: ACTION scenes: 0% intrusion. SUSPENSE scenes: 5% intrusion. NON-ACTION scenes: 15% intrusion at moments of weight; otherwise base voice.
forbidden_patterns: relative time references (a few days ago, several weeks later, etc.)
internal exposition dumps disguised as dialogue
lyrical or ornate metaphor (anti-Leonard) except a single sanctioned exception per book where explicitly directed in the outline
lush historical scene-setting paragraphs (the original Airmen saga register; the new run is leaner)

operational_doctrine: CSAR (Combat Search and Rescue) is the mission: recover downed airmen alive. The PJ creed governs — that others may live.
Jolly Green Two-Two operates out of Nakhon Phanom (NKP) Royal Thai Air Force Base, flying cross-border into Laos in support of the Ho Chi Minh Trail air war.
The Laos air war is officially deniable; US forces operate in a country the United States publicly says it is not fighting in.
A rescue package is Jolly Green (HH-3E rescue helicopter), Sandy (A-1 Skyraider RESCAP escort), and a Bird Dog FAC (forward air control). Sandy is the on-scene commander.
Pararescuemen (PJs) ride the hoist and the jungle penetrator down to recover survivors; they are medics first, operators second.
Radio reception over the karst and jungle is unreliable; communication breaks up, and transmitting can give away position.
universal_anti_patterns: AI-isms (it's not just X, it's Y / a testament to / in a world where)
redundant character reintroductions after the first appearance

recurring_characters.name: Danny Archer
recurring_characters.role: Protagonist (new run); USAF Pararescueman (PJ), Jolly Green Two-Two

recurring_characters.name: Tom Coyle
recurring_characters.role: Series co-lead (established Airmen canon); CIA contract pilot, 'Crash King'

recurring_characters.name: Rene Granier
recurring_characters.role: Series co-lead (established Airmen canon); CIA sniper, 'Buck'. Not in CSAR.

recurring_characters.name: Silas Vance
recurring_characters.role: Senior PJ, mentor to Archer, Jolly Green Two-Two ('Saint')

recurring_characters.name: Jack Dalton
recurring_characters.role: Helicopter pilot / aircraft commander, Jolly Green Two-Two ('Pappy')

recurring_characters.name: Meat Kowalski
recurring_characters.role: Lead flight engineer / crew chief, Jolly Green Two-Two

recurring_characters.name: Sparky Miller
recurring_characters.role: Flight engineer / scanner, Jolly Green Two-Two

world_rules: Series spans America's involvement in Vietnam from the 1945 OSS Deer Team (Book 1, 'A War Too Far') through the 1975 fall of Saigon ('The Beast Cometh').
The new twelve-book run follows Danny Archer's CSAR team; Coyle and Granier are the established series leads who appear across the run as legacy characters.
CSAR (Book 1 of the new run) is set in 1969, during the Ho Chi Minh Trail interdiction air war over Laos.
Coyle is the 'Crash King' — never bail if you can ride it down; the aircraft is the shell, the crew is the cargo.
Coyle's closest friend, James 'Earthquake McGoon' McGovern, was killed at the Siege of Dien Bien Phu (1954, Book 3 'We Stand Alone'); the loss haunts Coyle.
All books are historically accurate fiction — fictional characters set against documented historical events, in the manner of the established Airmen series ('Based on Historical Events').

historical_context.location: CSAR: Nakhon Phanom (NKP), Thailand and the Laotian panhandle (Khammouane province); the Ho Chi Minh Trail.
historical_context.scope: Twelve-book new run; CSAR is Book 1, a single ~90-hour rescue operation.

hard_constraints.setting_primary: Laotian panhandle jungle and karst (Khammouane province), 1969
setting_secondary_allowed: Nakhon Phanom (NKP) Royal Thai Air Force Base, Thailand
forbidden_settings: South Vietnam ground combat settings (this is the Laos air war)
Saigon / fall-of-Saigon material (belongs to 'The Beast Cometh', 1975)
Dien Bien Phu on-page (1954; backstory only, via Coyle's memory of McGoon)
hard_constraints.team_roster_constraint: These seven are the Jolly Green Two-Two crew. Tom Coyle is the rescued CIA pilot (not crew). Do NOT introduce additional named American operators. Support roles may appear unnamed or with first names only.
aircraft_canonical: HH-3E Jolly Green rescue helicopter ('Jolly Green Two-Two')
A-1 Skyraider ('Sandy', RESCAP escort / on-scene commander)
forbidden_aircraft: any aircraft not in service in 1969
weapons_canonical: Archer: PJ survival weapon and Ka-Bar knife
Pathet Lao/NVA: AK-47, RPD, RPG, SA-2 SAM
captured AK-47 recovered by Archer in Act 3
antagonists_canonical: Major Bounchanh Vorasak (Pathet Lao local commander)
Captain Tran Van Khoa (NVA General Reconnaissance Bureau intelligence officer)
forbidden_languages: any language not historically present in 1969 Laos
